title: Cracca incana

Cracca incana, also known as Psoralea incana, is a legume plant native to South Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, providing potential health benefits such as wound healing and protection against oxidative stress. Bioactive constituents of the plant include isoflavones, phenolic compounds, and alkaloids, which may contribute to its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Cracca incana include infusions, decoctions, and extracts, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin disorders and respiratory infections.
